food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — sandwich with baked dessert

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Subway Melt Cookie Meal combo. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is used as the closest parent for the sandwich component. The cookie component is not separately anchored.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKK (Sandwiches) is the closest parent for the sandwich component of this combo meal. No FoodEx2 code exists for a sandwich-plus-cookie combo meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a fast-food sandwich and cookie combo meal.

serving.description: 1 combo meal (6-inch Subway Melt sub + 1 cookie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 6-inch Subway Melt toasted sub (approximately 270 g) plus one Subway chocolate chip cookie (approximately 50 g), totalling approximately 320 g.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Melt 6-inch and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie — USDA Branded Food / Subway Nutrition Data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) — Subway Restaurants
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central branded entries for a 6-inch Subway Melt sub (USDA FDC NDB entries for Subway Melt) and a Subway chocolate chip cookie, then computing a weighted per-100g composite for the 320 g combo (270 g sub + 50 g cookie). Subway Melt 6-inch: ~410 kcal, 24 g protein, 14 g fat, 47 g carbohydrate per 270 g serving. Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie: ~220 kcal, 2 g protein, 10 g fat, 30 g carbohydrate per 50 g. Combined 320 g: ~630 kcal, 26 g protein, 24 g fat, 77 g carbohydrate. Per 100 g computed accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread and mixed sandwich analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Melt Cookie Meal combo. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries: white wheat bread sub roll GI ~70, deli meat and cheese fillings lower GI contribution, and chocolate chip cookie GI ~55. Weighted composite for the 320 g meal (270 g sub + 50 g cookie) yields an estimated blended GI of approximately 55. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 77.1) / 100 ≈ 42.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~1536 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 1536 mg sodium — over 65% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — primarily from processed deli meats, cheese, and condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load and low GI (estimated ~55) with high glycemic load (~42 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this meal with caution: the combination of a white-bread sub roll and a chocolate chip cookie delivers approximately 77 g of carbohydrate per serving with a glycemic load of ~42, which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, despite the GI being classified as low; portion reduction, substitution of a wholegrain roll, and omission of the cookie are recommended strategies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and ultra-processed meat components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is not recommended for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, as it contains processed deli meats, saturated fat (~9 g per serving), trace trans fat, and very high sodium (~1536 mg per serving), all of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Melt Cookie Meal is a fast-food combo meal sold at Subway restaurants in Singapore, consisting of a 6-inch Subway Melt toasted submarine sandwich paired with one Subway chocolate chip cookie. The Subway Melt sub features a toasted Italian or white wheat bread roll filled with a combination of deli meats — typically ham, salami, pepperoni, and turkey — layered with processed American-style cheese, and customisable with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, olives, and pickles, finished with condiments such as mayonnaise or mustard. The sandwich is toasted in a conveyor oven, giving the bread a warm, slightly crisp exterior while melting the cheese over the meats. The accompanying chocolate chip cookie is a soft-baked, sweet biscuit with a chewy texture and chocolate chip inclusions. As a combo, the meal delivers approximately 630 kcal, 26 g protein, 24 g fat, and 77 g carbohydrate per serving. The meal is ultra-processed, high in sodium (approximately 1536 mg per serving), and carries a low estimated glycemic index (~55) with a high glycemic load (~42), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is widely available across Subway outlets in Singapore and is a popular lunch or dinner option. The meal is halal-certified at Subway Singapore locations. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense and protein-moderate, but the refined carbohydrate content from the white roll and cookie, combined with the high sodium from processed meats and cheese, limits its suitability for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Subway Melt Cookie Meal?
Is Subway Melt Cookie Meal halal in Singapore?
What is in a Subway Melt sub?
Is Subway Melt Cookie Meal healthy?

confidence_reason: No exact branded entry for the Subway Melt Cookie Meal combo as sold in Singapore was found in a primary approved source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central branded entries for a 6-inch Subway Melt sub and a Subway chocolate chip cookie, then computing a weighted per-100g composite. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread sandwiches and chocolate chip cookies. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated composites derived from USDA FoodData Central branded entries for the Subway Melt 6-inch sub and Subway chocolate chip cookie; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact combo meal as sold in Singapore. 2. Serving size of 320 g is estimated (270 g sub + 50 g cookie); actual weights may vary by outlet and customisation. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA branded source entries used. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA branded source entries used. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 8. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 10. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 11. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 19. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 20. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 21. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 22. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source used. 23. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific combo meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 24. Halal status reflects Subway Singapore's halal certification; individual outlet status should be verified. 25. The food_group has been set to Mixed (overriding the input Desserts) as this is a combo meal containing both a savoury sandwich and a dessert cookie component.
